lines.text: The city sleeps beneath obsidian skies
A thousand lights dissolving in the rain
I drive alone on roads without a name
Where shadows dance and silence never dies
There is a place between the dark and dawn
Where all the things I never said live on
Obsidian — cold and still and vast
A mirror holding everything I've passed
I keep my hands at ten and two
The radio plays something I once knew
Another mile, another ghost
Another city I have loved the most
But the road goes on — and so do I
Beneath the weight of an obsidian sky
Carbon black on carbon black
Engine hum — no turning back
The highway bends toward morning light
I am the driver, I am the night
Speed is just another word for free
The road knows what I cannot see
Carbon lines on carbon roads
Carrying these unnamed loads
Every exit passed is a choice I made
Every shadow crossed is a debt I've paid
I don't look back — I don't look down
Carbon drive through a carbon town
Somewhere in the velvet between breaths
A note is held so long it becomes silence
And silence becomes the thing itself
All the underground rivers are music
We just don't have ears low enough to hear
Press your palm to the floor
Listen with the whole body
This is what velvet sounds like
This is the underground
Rain on the window like a jazz drummer
Playing only for himself
The cognac settles in the glass
Golden and still and deep
You were here once in this chair
Reading something with a green spine
The rain doesn't remember you
But I do
Cognac and rain
That's all memory is sometimes
A warmth and a window
And the sound of things falling
Everyone was searching
I was just moving
And the gold found me on the road
Not in a river — in a decision
Gold rush is not a place
It is a tempo
It is the speed at which you stop hesitating
And start becoming
Rush
Gold
Rush
0:00 — initiate the protocol
The midnight window is open for twelve minutes
After that, the city locks itself
Move now or dissolve into the crowd
Midnight protocol — no names exchanged
Only coordinates and instinct
You've trained for this in every dream
Wake up — this is the real version
The protocol has begun
There is no abort
Every cipher has a key
The key is not a word — it is a frequency
Tune yourself to the right signal
And the locked doors will open without handles
I have been decoding this city for years
Pattern behind pattern behind pattern
The cipher is simple once you stop
Trying to read it with your eyes
Depth is not silence
Depth is everything at once, balanced
The ocean floor is not empty
It is the loudest place on earth
If you listen without agenda
The deep focus comes to you
You stop chasing it
And it arrives like water finding its level
Still
Here
After the storm the light has a different weight
Heavier somehow
Like it carries everything the rain washed clean
I stood at the window and watched it end
The city steaming in the sudden quiet
After the storm I felt something leave me
Something I had been holding since winter
And I was lighter for it
Not empty — lighter
The way a room feels after you open every window
There is static between every beautiful thing
The champagne fizzes — that is static
The space between your words and mine — that too
We call some static music
And some we call silence
And some we call the evening
The champagne static settles into taste
And we drink it like it's the last glass
Because every glass is the last glass
If you're paying attention
Grey afternoon stretched without corners
The tea went cold before I noticed
That is grief — the cold tea
The moment you missed without knowing
Not the absence but the forgetting of absence
Grey afternoon
I don't need it to end
I just need to be inside it correctly
Iron will is not stubbornness
Iron will is choosing your fight
And then refusing to be moved from it
No one gave you this
You forged it
In the hours before anyone else was awake
In the repetitions no one saw
Iron doesn't come from wanting it
Iron comes from heat and pressure and time
You have all three
